expert_rss.title: 1-40th Cavalry Regiment Prepares for Final Transformation During Exercise Red Flag
expert_rss.summary: Soldiers assigned to the 2nd Infantry Brigade Combat Team, 11th Airborne Division, at Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson, Alaska, demonstrated the Army's commitment to transformation, readiness and joint lethality during Exercise Red Flag-Alaska 26-2.

expert_rss.title: Naval Research Laboratory Receives Space Force Antenna, Expanding Joint Space Capabilities
expert_rss.summary: The U.S. Naval Research Laboratory received a transportable satellite tracking antenna system from Space Systems Command's System Delta 81 to expand joint space testing, training and operational support capabilities at the laboratory's Blossom Point Tracking Facility in Welcome, Md.

expert_rss.title: Michigan Army National Guard Uses UAS to Train for Tomorrow's Battlefield
expert_rss.summary: Nine soldiers assigned to the 3rd Battalion, 126th Infantry Regiment, participated in a two-week unmanned aerial system program, as the Michigan National Guard expands its drone training initiative.

expert_rss.title: Carrying the Torch: 815th Airlift Squadron Assumes Airlift Mission in Africa
expert_rss.summary: Airmen assigned to the Minnesota Air National Guard 133rd Airlift Wing concluded a historic deployment to Africa, as airmen assigned to the Air Force Reserve 815th Airlift Squadron, known as the Flying Jennies, are carrying the torch for the next rotation.

expert_rss.title: Task Force Civil Affairs Team Conducts Subject Matter Expert Exchange in Libya
expert_rss.summary: U.S. Army civil affairs soldiers conducted a military-to-military subject matter expert exchange with representatives of the Libyan National Army and the Government of National Unity in Benghazi, Libya.

expert_rss.title: A-10 Warthogs Arrive In England Festooned With Nose Art And Mission Markings From Epic Fury

wikipedia.notes: Shortly after 8:00 a.m. PKT, an explosives-laden vehicle struck one of the carriages of a shuttle train near Chaman Phatak as the train was travelling through Quetta.

An individual with a known history of mental issues and support of Islamic State stabbed and injured three people at the Winterthur railway station while shouting Allahu Akbar. The Zurich canton police described the incident as an act of terrorism.
